Both delegations have now shared written demands and possible compromises, positioning the second round with "clearer positions" than the first meeting.[5]

The lack of a breakthrough in the initial talks, combined with reported ceasefire violations and continued disagreement over fundamental issues (nuclear enrichment, sanctions relief, military withdrawal), suggests protracted negotiations ahead
